name: Paper House — Four Rooms at Night
description: A scroll-through WebGL diorama of four small rooms that share a central corner. Each room has its own song (30s Apple Music preview), two lines of hand-composed lyrics in the user's language, falling word confetti, custom decoration stickers, a character figure, and ONE clickable hero item that opens a handwritten memory card. Originally built around couple memory anchors; adapts to any relationship where one evening-or-period can be split into four scenes.
scenes_note: These are the ORIGINAL scene ids. The 4 scenes can be re-themed per gift (library / supermarket / park / playground / studio / subway-car, etc.) as long as each is a 'small-room-like' space. When the scene theme changes, you MUST keep the scene IDs (kitchen/rooftop/karaoke/couch) because they are referenced throughout the template HTML; only swap the visual content, palette, song, lyrics, decor, figure, and hero item. Treat scene id as a stable slot key, not as semantic content.

room_geometry.architecture: 4 quadrants sharing a single central corner (0,0,0). Each room occupies one horizontal quadrant with a LEFT radial wall and a RIGHT radial wall; the inner corner of every room is the same point, and adjacent rooms share their common wall.
room_geometry.must_preserve: Do NOT change WALL_LEN / WALL_H / WALL_THK / ROOF_Y / ROOM_Y_OFFSET / room positions / shared-corner topology. The camera logic, snap angles, sticker coordinate system, and lyric layout all assume this geometry.
room_geometry.wall_texture_role: The left and right walls and the floor are FLAT rectangular textures — they clothe the wall surface. Do not produce '3D room scenes' as wall textures. The texture IS the wall; perspective already comes from the 3D scene. Three-dimensional detail (a window frame, a picture on the wall, a hanging lamp) MAY be painted into the wall texture, but depth beyond the wall surface (a room receding into the distance) must not.

slots.purpose: Optional per-scene re-theming. Default is the original kitchen/rooftop/karaoke/couch. May be changed when the recipient brief strongly suggests a different four-scene split (e.g. library/bookshop/park/café for a reading-partner gift). The id stays the same; only the visual & textual theming changes.

output_fields_per_scene.theme: one short word describing the re-themed room, e.g. 'kitchen' / 'library' / 'boba-shop'
output_fields_per_scene.eyebrow: small kicker line shown in UI, e.g. a date or phrase
output_fields_per_scene.note: 1-2 lines of narrator-voice text that frames the room's feeling

components_per_scene.prompt_role: the LEFT radial wall of this room — flat wall content only
the RIGHT radial wall of this room — flat wall content only; must feel like the same room as the left wall
top-down floor texture for this room (tile / rug / wood / concrete etc., whatever matches the scene)

physical_rules: A wall image shows ONLY what would be painted/hung on that wall surface. No receding-into-space perspective.
3D detail painted onto the wall (window frame with visible depth, picture in a frame, hanging lamp, clock, shelf against the wall) is welcome and makes the room feel real.
Left and right walls must read as part of ONE cohesive room — same lighting direction, same palette, same style, same era.
Floor texture is viewed from above. It usually tiles or reads as a continuous ground plane.
slots.style_anchor: template-source/scene_{scene_id}.jpg (reference image — mood, palette, painterly storybook feel)
slots.style_hint_default: painterly storybook diorama, intimate human-scale lighting, slight film grain, medium saturation, paper-cutout feel rather than photoreal. Color palette must follow the per-scene palette defined in ROOMS[scene].palette.

slots.purpose: One to two figure sprites per room showing the protagonist(s) inside that scene. The figures are cut-out silhouette-like stickers that occlude characters with scene context (the user cooking / standing on the rooftop / singing / on the couch). Look at FIGURE_LAYOUT in the canonical HTML for positioning intent.
identity_rules: If the user provided reference photos of the recipient (and optionally themselves), the AI-generated figure MUST preserve recognizable facial structure, hair, skin tone, and clothing style from the reference photo. Not merely 'a person' but 'this person'.
If no reference photo is available, generate consistent but non-specific figures across all 4 rooms — same body proportions, same clothing palette — so the viewer reads them as the same character(s).
Support 1 or 2 figures per scene. The rooftop in the canonical template has 2 figures; the others have 1.

params.note: Applied only when this is a direct user photo; skip when it's an AI-generated scene-figure composition that already reads as a cutout.

placement_rules: Place decorations inside each room's quadrant using the DECOR_LAYOUT schema in the canonical HTML (x/y/z/s/order/rot). Use the canonical layout as the starting point; only re-pick which sticker fills each slot based on user context.
Some stickers MAY intentionally occlude the character figure in the foreground — this is an allowed depth trick and makes the diorama feel layered.
If a sticker is a photo frame, picture frame, or any container that is meant to hold content, you must ALSO place a separate inner-content sticker on top of it — never deliver an empty frame.
slots.hero_item_rule: Exactly ONE sticker per room is the hero interactive item — the one users click to open the memory card. See `scene_hero_items` for the schema. Pick a hero sticker whose semantics clearly map to a memory the user shared.

slots.popup_shape: Matches STICKER_INTERACTIONS in the canonical HTML: each popup is a handwritten memory card with a title, kicker, background story-card image, paper color, accent color, overlay decals, and a handwritten text block.

popup_contract.title: short CJK title for the memory, 6-14 characters
popup_contract.kicker: short room/item label, e.g. '厨房 / 冰箱门'
popup_contract.image: path to a generated story card (see story_cards slot)
popup_contract.decals: 3-4 small overlay stickers around the card, each with src/x/y/w/r, positioned via percentage inside the card frame
popup_contract.text: a handwritten-tone memory note addressed TO the recipient, in the user's primary language. 60-110 characters. Grounded in a specific detail from the user's intake material. Never generic; never guilt-tripping. See gifting-ethics.md.

slots.generation_contract: Use the same identity-preserve + compositing approach documented in `template-source/story_cards/prompts/story_card_image_prompts.json`. Each card combines the protagonist reference photos, the clicked sticker, the room's mood-reference image, and 2-3 supporting sticker references. Must leave generous clean blank space in a specific region (right / lower-right / etc.) for a handwritten CJK text overlay. No readable text inside the image. No watermark, no extra people.
physical_rules: The hero object (fridge, suitcase, camera, chess board) must be the visual center and correctly rendered — e.g. a fridge's door must connect to the fridge body; a chess board must have visible grid; a suitcase must have handle/hinges in the right places.
The protagonist(s) must be visible and recognizably the same people as the room figures. The same identity reference propagates to all 4 story cards.
Palette and lighting must match the corresponding room's palette (see ROOMS[scene].palette in the canonical HTML).
The scene must tell a small self-contained micro-moment involving the hero object and the people — not just the object alone, and not just the people alone.
slots.model_hint: Use a model that supports multi-image identity preservation. The existing pipeline uses `gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview` via litellm (see template-source/scripts/generate_story_cards_litellm.py).

contract.input_to_ai: Per-scene mood + user memories + user-provided song hints (if any). If the user provided a specific song for this scene, use it. Otherwise, AI picks a song whose tone matches the scene's emotional beat from the recipient brief.
contract.search_strategy: Use the iTunes Search API (https://itunes.apple.com/search?term=<title>+<artist>&entity=song&limit=5) to resolve a candidate song to its 30s preview URL. Apple exposes these preview URLs publicly, so no auth key is required.

fields_written_to_html.title: song title (any language)
fields_written_to_html.previewUrl: the 30-second m4a preview URL returned by iTunes Search API

slots.copyright_rule: Only the 30s preview URL (hosted by Apple) is embedded. Do NOT download and re-host the preview audio. Do NOT generate or quote large spans of original copyrighted lyrics. See scene_lyrics for how lyrics are written.

slots.purpose: Two long prose-lyric sentences per scene, used for the wavy text banner at the top of each room. These are the user's narrator-voice reflections, NOT the song's lyrics.
composition_rules: Each scene has two sentences. Each sentence is roughly 80-140 CJK characters when the user's language is Chinese; calibrate proportionally for other languages.

The sentence MAY include ONE short phrase (up to ~12 CJK chars) lightly quoted from the song's lyrics in the form 'X's song «Song Title» 里那句「quoted phrase」' — this is the fair-use-sized reference, not a bulk quotation.

The remaining ~95% of the sentence is ORIGINAL text written in the user's voice: it anchors on concrete memories the user shared during intake. Every sentence must contain at least one specific detail that traces to the user's intake material.
Tone: intimate, present-tense, addressed to TA (second person) OR reflective about TA (third person) — pick per-gift based on whose point of view reads most naturally.

NEVER generate full verse/chorus rewrites. NEVER approximate or lightly paraphrase multiple lines of any copyrighted song. If the user wants a song's lyric to carry the gift, quote at most ~12 chars from it and build original text around that quote.

layout_rules.font_consistency: The two sentence lines and the scene's falling words must all use the SAME font family and same base size; glow intensity must be identical. Do not make the falling words larger/smaller or differently styled.
layout_rules.baseline_undulation: Each sentence is laid out along a gentle wave. The middle portion of each sentence (near the center of the banner / room) should be flatter — closer to horizontal. The left and right edges should have more pronounced rise/fall. Think of a shallow wave that dips harder at the ends.
layout_rules.horizontal_asymmetry: The characters' horizontal placements must be slightly varied — NOT evenly spaced. Introduce small lateral jitter so the line reads as handwritten-on-a-wave, not as typeset. Avoid any row that reads as a perfect rectangle.
layout_rules.rotation: The sentence itself stays upright; only individual FALLING words (below) rotate.

slots.purpose: Short phrases that fall like confetti from the banner area.
rules: Each fall-word MUST be a complete meaningful phrase — 2-6 characters typically, sometimes up to 10. Never a broken fragment of a longer thought.
Source: mix of (a) user-memory-derived specifics ('番茄香气', '你回头', '递过碗') and (b) general mood tokens ('很轻很久', '再靠近一点'). Roughly 60% specific, 40% mood.
Each falling word is rendered with a random rotation angle in [-20°, +20°] — never 0°, never > 20°. The rotation is per-word, random each page load is fine.
Fonts, sizes, and glow exactly match the main scene_lyrics band — see `font_consistency` rule there.

required_for_resource_delivery_parsing: (uses python stdlib zipfile + xml.etree; no package needed)
python_packages.notes: Install on demand in a local venv when running the template. See SPEC.md § Dependencies.

rembg_models.download_location: rembg auto-downloads to ~/.u2net by default, or to DEAR_REMBG_MODEL_HOME if set
rembg_models.notes: First-time cutout takes a minute while the model downloads. Subsequent runs are fast.

itunes_search.rate_limit: ~20 requests / minute per IP (best-effort; use sparingly)

image_generation.config_source: The existing script expects litellm BaseUrl + Api key in a docx at /Users/liujiawei/Desktop/资源交付.docx. For re-use in the skill, the agent should either (a) read the same docx on macOS locally, or (b) pass LITELLM_BASE_URL + LITELLM_API_KEY env vars. See SPEC.md § Dependencies for fallback paths including direct Gemini API and OpenRouter.

bundle_contents: reference/{room}/1..6.png + player.png   (example room sprite set — layout reference)

build_contract.inline_strategy: base64 all images directly into HTML (build.py already does this)
